summary: WIN-067 finding (and tldr_evidence / tldr_verdict for consistency) is rewritten to disclose that the 1,430× headline is the apples-to-apples ratio under linear g↔H(r) coupling, brackets it with ~590× under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling, and preserves 170× as a dimensionless lower bound from the whole-globe pole-equator spread. The new finding reads: 'Dome\'s own H(r) implies ≥38% gravity drop at the Antarctic rim under any monotonic g↔H(r) coupling: 1,430× apples-to-apples error under linear coupling, ~590× under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling, with 170× as a dimensionless lower bound from the whole-globe pole-equator spread.' This closes c6 hole H3 — the c6 advocate's 'I never published linear coupling' move now lands on text that explicitly bounds every plausible monotonic coupling. EXP-215 contains the full replacement strings for finding, tldr_evidence, and tldr_verdict (Patches 1–3) plus exact patch instructions for the decider.
